note: Accessible building with level entry, lifts, and accessible parking; the grounds and terraces offer skyline and Hollywood Sign views. The lawn is firm. Free entry, paid parking.
Fully accessible with a tram from the parking structure up to the museum, lifts throughout, loaner wheelchairs and free entry. Gardens have mostly accessible paths. Superb for all.
The pier is largely accessible and the beachfront Marvin Braude path is flat and paved; beach wheelchairs and accessible mats are available. Easy and pleasant.
Flat, wide sidewalks make the stars easy to stroll and wheel along, though crowded and gritty in stretches. Step-free throughout.
Modern campus with step-free galleries, lifts and accessible entrances; the Urban Light installation is on flat ground. Easy.
A flat, paved beachfront boardwalk that's fully step-free and lively; sandy sections aside, the main path is easy to wheel or push.

place: Griffith Park hikes / Hollywood Sign trail
note: The trails to viewpoints and the sign are steep, unpaved fire roads with no accessible route; not suitable for wheelchairs, strollers or limited mobility. Alt: drive to the Griffith Observatory for the view.
Free contemporary museum with full lift access and step-free galleries; timed reservations. Very accessible.
A flat, ground-level historic food hall; aisles are navigable though busy at peak meals. Accessible with some tight moments.
The park provides accessibility passes and most areas are navigable, but the site is built on a hillside with escalators and long slopes; a lot of distance and gradient. Partly tiring.
The striking exterior and adjacent gardens are on flat, step-free ground with ramped access; easy to visit and photograph. Interior tours vary.
